seoTitle: Clarence Gardens vs Clarence Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Clarence Gardens and Clarence Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Clarence Gardens vs Clarence Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Clarence Gardens and Clarence Park.
heading: Clarence Gardens vs Clarence Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Clarence Gardens, South Australia and Clarence Park, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clarence Gardens has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Clarence Park (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Clarence Gardens or Clarence Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Clarence Gardens has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Clarence Park scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Clarence Gardens scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Clarence Park scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Clarence Gardens or Clarence Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clarence Gardens has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
